name: Peru
avoid: January–February rains can trigger landslides and train delays around Cusco, Sacred Valley, and Machu Picchu
Do not stack heavy activity immediately after arrival in Cusco or Puno — altitude hits fast above 3,300 m

cities.name: Lima
cities.notes: Food-first capital with Miraflores cliffs, Barranco street art, Larco Museum and easy long-haul recovery before the Andes

cities.name: Cusco
cities.notes: Acclimatization base for Inca walls, Plaza de Armas, San Pedro Market and the Sacsayhuamán archaeological circuit

cities.name: Sacred Valley
cities.notes: Urubamba-Ollantaytambo-Pisac corridor for ruins, markets, Moray terraces and Maras salt pans

cities.name: Machu Picchu
cities.notes: Aguas Calientes gateway for the citadel, dawn entry circuits and optional Huayna Picchu hike

cities.name: Arequipa
cities.notes: White-stone colonial city with Santa Catalina Monastery, volcano views and the classic route into Colca Canyon

cities.name: Lake Titicaca
cities.notes: Puno-side base for Uros floating islands, Taquile textiles and high-altitude lake culture

itinerary.sim: Claro or Entel prepago/eSIM is easiest after landing in Lima; expect roughly ₹920–₹1,380 for 10–15GB. Download offline maps before heading into the Sacred Valley and on the rail segment because coverage gets patchy beyond Ollantaytambo.

Lima.name: Lima
Lima.note: Use Miraflores or Barranco as your base, keep a buffer for international arrival day, and save the heaviest altitude days for after Lima.

activities.name: Walk Parque del Amor, Malecón de Miraflores and Larcomar on the Pacific cliffs
Museo Larco
Huaca Pucllana site museum and adobe pyramid
Barranco stroll via Puente de los Suspiros and Bajada de Baños
Plaza Mayor, Cathedral exterior and changing of the guard zone walk
Monastery of San Francisco catacombs
MALI - Museo de Arte de Lima
Evening domestic flight to Cusco on LATAM or SKY if you want to maximize Andes time

Cusco.name: Cusco
Cusco.note: Treat day one as acclimatization, keep coca tea or hydration handy, and book Machu Picchu plus train slots before you arrive in Cusco.

activities.name: Plaza de Armas, Twelve-Angled Stone and San Blas lanes
Qorikancha Temple and Convent of Santo Domingo
San Pedro Market snacks, coffee and light lunch
Cusco Cathedral interior
Boleto Turístico del Cusco partial circuit for Sacsayhuamán, Qenqo, Puka Pukara and Tambomachay
Taxi or guided loop through the four archaeological sites
Mirador desde Cristo Blanco for sunset over Cusco

days.theme: Slow Morning or Extra Museum Buffer

activities.name: Centro de Textiles Tradicionales del Cusco or Museo Inka
Pre-buy snacks and pack a light day bag for the Sacred Valley and Machu Picchu rail segment
Optional massage or spa session to stay fresh before train travel

Sacred Valley.name: Sacred Valley
Sacred Valley.note: Stay near Ollantaytambo or Urubamba so the train day is easier, and use the full tourist ticket if you plan multiple valley ruins.

activities.name: Transfer from Cusco by colectivo or taxi into Pisac or Urubamba
Boleto Turístico Integral for Cusco plus Sacred Valley sites
Pisac archaeological park and market lanes
Slow evening in Urubamba with valley views
Moray circular terraces using the tourist ticket
Maras salt mines community ticket
Ollantaytambo fortress and old Inca town streets
Optional evening PeruRail or IncaRail departure to Aguas Calientes

Machu Picchu.name: Machu Picchu
Machu Picchu.note: Book the circuit first, then train, then hotel; aim for one of the earlier entry windows and pack only the small bag allowed on the train.

days.theme: Aguas Calientes Arrival & Hot Springs Town

activities.name: PeruRail or IncaRail ride from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes
Mandor waterfall trail or butterfly garden if you reach early
Town walk along the Urubamba river and early ticket check for the next morning
Consettur shuttle bus up and down between Aguas Calientes and the citadel
Machu Picchu Llaqta entry - Circuit 2 or equivalent main circuit
Optional Huayna Picchu or Machu Picchu Mountain permit when available
Return rail to Ollantaytambo or Cusco after the visit

Arequipa.name: Arequipa
Arequipa.note: Arequipa is lower than Cusco but still elevated, so keep hydration up and decide early whether you want a long Colca day trip or a proper overnight canyon stay.

activities.name: Santa Catalina Monastery
Plaza de Armas, Basilica Cathedral exterior and Claustros de la Compañía
Museo Santuarios Andinos to see the Juanita exhibit
Yanahuara viewpoint at sunset
Shared Colca Canyon tour from Arequipa via Chivay and Cruz del Condor
Colca Canyon tourist ticket
Optional La Calera hot springs stop
Evening bus or flight onward if you are compressing the route

Lake Titicaca.name: Lake Titicaca
Lake Titicaca.note: Puno is cold after sunset even in the dry season, and lake trips feel better if you leave one lighter afternoon before or after a long bus ride.

activities.name: Full-day speedboat or regular boat tour to Uros floating islands and Taquile Island
Optional reed-boat ride at Uros
Taquile community fee and textile demonstrations
Sillustani funerary towers en route to or from Juliaca
Juliaca airport transfer for LATAM or SKY flight back to Lima
Optional evening folk show at Balcones de Puno

meals: Cafe Bar de la Casa del Corregidor

connections.method: LATAM or SKY domestic flight into Alejandro Velasco Astete Airport, about 1 hr 20 min
Taxi, private transfer, or colectivo via Pisac or Chinchero; Ollantaytambo is usually 1.5–2 hrs from Cusco
PeruRail or IncaRail from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes; most convenient same-day rail link
Return by train to Ollantaytambo or Cusco, then continue by LATAM or SKY flight to Arequipa; overnight Cruz del Sur is the budget fallback
Cruz del Sur bus from Arequipa to Puno in roughly 6–7 hrs; private transfer is faster but pricier

extras: Book Machu Picchu tickets before trains and hotels, especially for May–September when the better morning circuits sell out first.
Cusco, Colca, and Puno all sit at serious altitude, so keep the first 24 hours light and avoid stacking a canyon day right after landing if you can.
Carry some cash in soles for site tickets, taxis, market snacks, and Uros add-ons because card acceptance still drops outside the bigger hotels.
PeruRail and IncaRail both enforce small-bag limits on many services, so leave heavy luggage at your Cusco or Sacred Valley hotel when possible.
If time is tight, keep Lima, Cusco, Sacred Valley, and Machu Picchu as the non-negotiable core; add Arequipa and Lake Titicaca only if you have the extra transit days.
